info.name: FirstClub Membership API
info.description:
Complete REST surface of the FirstClub Membership backend (Spring Boot 4.1 / Java 21 / Postgres).

## Get it running (about 3 minutes)

From the repo root:

```
cp .env.example .env      # REQUIRED - JWT_SECRET has no default; the app will not boot without it
docker compose up -d      # Postgres
mvn spring-boot:run       # Flyway migrates + seeds on startup
```

Then run this collection's **Auth** folder first - it mints and stores both tokens for you.

If the app exits with `Migrations have failed validation`, the Postgres volume predates the current schema. Reset it: `docker compose down -v && docker compose up -d`.

## Before you start

The app requires two environment variables or it will not start / will not mint tokens:

- `JWT_SECRET` — HS256 signing key, >= 32 bytes. No default, deliberately.
- `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_SECRET` — required to mint an ADMIN token. Blank means admin minting is impossible.

Also set `AUTH_DEV_TOKEN_ENABLED=true` and `AUTH_DEV_TOKEN_ALLOW_OUTSIDE_DEV=true` (both default off) or `POST /api/v1/auth/token` will not exist. `.env.example` in the repo sets all of these for local runs.

## Run order

Run the folders top to bottom. `Auth` captures both tokens, `Catalog` captures `planId` / `tierId` / `goldTierId` / `platinumTierId`, and `Subscriptions > Subscribe` captures `subscriptionId` — nothing needs to be copied by hand.

## Authorization model

- **Public** (no token): `POST /api/v1/auth/token`, `GET /api/v1/plans`, `GET /api/v1/tiers`, `/actuator/health`, `/actuator/info`, Swagger and `/v3/api-docs`.
- **ADMIN only**: `/api/v1/admin/**`, `GET`/`POST /api/v1/users`, activity ingestion, subscription refund, and any non-liveness actuator endpoint enabled later (only health/info are exposed today).
- **Self-or-admin**: `GET /api/v1/users/{id}`, membership, credit notes, and the subscription mutations (subscribe / upgrade / downgrade / cancel).

## Error contract

All errors are RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`. 404 not found, 409 conflict (including concurrency and illegal lifecycle transitions), 422 domain-rule violation, 402 payment declined, 400 bean-validation failure.

variable.description: Root of the running service. Override in the environment file if SERVER_PORT differs.
JWT for a normal member. Populated by 'Auth > Mint self token'.
JWT carrying ROLE_ADMIN. Populated by 'Auth > Mint admin token'.
Seeded demo user Aarav Shah (no cohort). Other seeds: 7002 Priya Nair (PREMIER), 7003 Rohan Verma (VIP), 7004 Meera Iyer.
Id of the Monthly plan. Populated by 'Catalog > List plans'.
Id of the base tier (rank 1 = SILVER). Populated by 'Catalog > List tiers'.
Id of the rank-2 tier (GOLD). Populated by 'Catalog > List tiers'.
Id of the rank-3 tier (PLATINUM). Populated by 'Catalog > List tiers'.
Populated by 'Subscriptions > Subscribe to a plan at a tier'.
Must equal the app's ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_SECRET. This is the value in .env.example.
Generated from {{$guid}} on the first Subscribe send and reused by the replay request, so the idempotent-retry demo is reproducible.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Dev JWT issuance — a stand-in for a real IdP, gated by `membership.auth.dev-token.enabled`. Both requests are **public** (no Authorization header): `/api/v1/auth/**` is `permitAll()` in SecurityConfig.

request.description:
Issues a member JWT whose subject is `userId`. That subject is exactly what every `@PreAuthorize` self-or-admin check compares against.

**Auth: public** — no token required.

No `secret` is needed for a non-admin token, and omitting `admin` means "no" (the field is a boxed `Boolean` so the minimal payload `{"userId":7001}` is valid rather than a 400).

The test script stores the token in the `selfToken` collection variable — run this first.

originalRequest.description:
Issues a member JWT whose subject is `userId`. That subject is exactly what every `@PreAuthorize` self-or-admin check compares against.

**Auth: public** — no token required.

No `secret` is needed for a non-admin token, and omitting `admin` means "no" (the field is a boxed `Boolean` so the minimal payload `{"userId":7001}` is valid rather than a 400).

The test script stores the token in the `selfToken` collection variable — run this first.

exec: pm.test('200 OK — bootstrap secret accepted', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
console.warn('403: adminBootstrapSecret does not match the app ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_SECRET, or it is blank (admin minting disabled).');

request.description:
Issues a JWT carrying ROLE_ADMIN.

**Auth: public**, but `admin: true` **requires** `secret` to match the app's `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_SECRET` (compared in constant time), so admin escalation is never anonymous. A blank/unset secret on the server makes admin minting impossible and this returns **403**.

The test script stores the token in the `adminToken` collection variable.

originalRequest.description:
Issues a JWT carrying ROLE_ADMIN.

**Auth: public**, but `admin: true` **requires** `secret` to match the app's `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_SECRET` (compared in constant time), so admin escalation is never anonymous. A blank/unset secret on the server makes admin minting impossible and this returns **403**.

The test script stores the token in the `adminToken` collection variable.

item.name: Catalog
item.description: Read-only, in-memory cached catalog. Both endpoints are **public** — SecurityConfig `permitAll()`s `GET /api/v1/plans` and `GET /api/v1/tiers`, so these requests deliberately send **no** Authorization header.

exec: var monthly = plans.filter(function (p) { return p.cadence === 'MONTHLY'; })[0] || plans[0];

request.description:
Lists the active membership plans. Seeded: **Monthly** 199.00 / 30 days, **Quarterly** 499.00 / 90 days, **Yearly** 1499.00 / 365 days. Each `PlanDto` is `{id, cadence, name, price, durationDays, trialDays}`.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

The test script captures the Monthly plan's id into `planId`.

originalRequest.description:
Lists the active membership plans. Seeded: **Monthly** 199.00 / 30 days, **Quarterly** 499.00 / 90 days, **Yearly** 1499.00 / 365 days. Each `PlanDto` is `{id, cadence, name, price, durationDays, trialDays}`.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

The test script captures the Monthly plan's id into `planId`.

exec: pm.test('tiers are returned with benefits', function () {
var hit = tiers.filter(function (t) { return t.rank === rank; })[0];
var base = idOfRank(1), gold = idOfRank(2), platinum = idOfRank(3);
console.log('tierId(SILVER)=' + base + ' goldTierId=' + gold + ' platinumTierId=' + platinum);

request.description:
Lists active tiers with the benefits each unlocks and their effective params.

Seeded tiers, ascending rank: **SILVER** (1), **GOLD** (2), **PLATINUM** (3). Benefit types are `FREE_DELIVERY`, `EXTRA_DISCOUNT`, `EXCLUSIVE_DEALS`, `EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS`, `PRIORITY_SUPPORT`; higher tiers widen the same perks via params (e.g. `{"discountPct":5}` on Silver vs `{"discountPct":15}` on Platinum) rather than gaining separate rows.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

The test script captures `tierId` (rank 1), `goldTierId` (rank 2) and `platinumTierId` (rank 3).

response.name: 200 — tiers with perks

originalRequest.description:
Lists active tiers with the benefits each unlocks and their effective params.

Seeded tiers, ascending rank: **SILVER** (1), **GOLD** (2), **PLATINUM** (3). Benefit types are `FREE_DELIVERY`, `EXTRA_DISCOUNT`, `EXCLUSIVE_DEALS`, `EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS`, `PRIORITY_SUPPORT`; higher tiers widen the same perks via params (e.g. `{"discountPct":5}` on Silver vs `{"discountPct":15}` on Platinum) rather than gaining separate rows.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

The test script captures `tierId` (rank 1), `goldTierId` (rank 2) and `platinumTierId` (rank 3).

item.name: Users
item.description: User directory. Listing and provisioning are **ADMIN-only** (explicit matchers in SecurityConfig); reading a single user is **self-or-admin** via `@PreAuthorize`.

request.description:
Lists the user directory, paginated. Returns `UserDto{id, name, email, cohort}`.

**Auth: ADMIN** — `GET /api/v1/users` is matched explicitly as `hasRole('ADMIN')`, so a member token gets 403.

Seeded users: 7001 Aarav Shah, 7002 Priya Nair (PREMIER), 7003 Rohan Verma (VIP), 7004 Meera Iyer.

response.name: 200 — seeded directory

originalRequest.description:
Lists the user directory, paginated. Returns `UserDto{id, name, email, cohort}`.

**Auth: ADMIN** — `GET /api/v1/users` is matched explicitly as `hasRole('ADMIN')`, so a member token gets 403.

Seeded users: 7001 Aarav Shah, 7002 Priya Nair (PREMIER), 7003 Rohan Verma (VIP), 7004 Meera Iyer.

request.description:
Fetches one user from the directory.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#id.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`. The self token works because its subject is `{{userId}}`; asking for someone else's id with the same token returns 403.

Unknown id returns **404** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`.

originalRequest.description:
Fetches one user from the directory.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#id.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`. The self token works because its subject is `{{userId}}`; asking for someone else's id with the same token returns 403.

Unknown id returns **404** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`.

request.description:
Provisions a new user in the directory. Responds **201 Created** with the `UserDto`.

**Auth: ADMIN** — `POST /api/v1/users` is matched explicitly as `hasRole('ADMIN')`.

Body is `CreateUserRequest{name, email, cohort}`: `name` and `email` are `@NotBlank` and `email` is `@Email`, so a malformed payload returns **400** with a problem+json listing the field errors. `cohort` is optional and feeds the cohort tier rule (`PREMIER` qualifies for Gold, `VIP` for Platinum). Re-sending the same email conflicts on the unique index and surfaces as **409**.

originalRequest.description:
Provisions a new user in the directory. Responds **201 Created** with the `UserDto`.

**Auth: ADMIN** — `POST /api/v1/users` is matched explicitly as `hasRole('ADMIN')`.

Body is `CreateUserRequest{name, email, cohort}`: `name` and `email` are `@NotBlank` and `email` is `@Email`, so a malformed payload returns **400** with a problem+json listing the field errors. `cohort` is optional and feeds the cohort tier rule (`PREMIER` qualifies for Gold, `VIP` for Platinum). Re-sending the same email conflicts on the unique index and surfaces as **409**.

item.name: Subscriptions
item.description:
Subscription lifecycle plus the membership and credit-note read views.

Every mutation moves money or revokes access, so each carries an explicit `@PreAuthorize`: subscribe checks the `userId` in the body, the path-addressed mutations check the subscription's owner, and refund is ADMIN-only. `isOwner` returns false both for "not yours" and "does not exist", so an unauthorised caller gets an identical **403** either way and cannot enumerate subscription ids.

All mutations accept an optional `Idempotency-Key`; a stable key makes a retry replay the original result instead of re-mutating.

Lifecycle note: `CANCELLED` and `EXPIRED` are terminal. Cancel and refund are therefore mutually exclusive — whichever you run second returns **409 Illegal Subscription State**.

item.name: Subscribe to a plan at a tier

exec: // Generate the idempotency key once and keep it, so the replay request below can
// resend the SAME key and demonstrate the retry-safety guarantee end to end.
pm.test('subscription is ACTIVE at the requested tier', function () {
console.log('subscriptionId = ' + sub.id + ' (' + sub.planCadence + ' / ' + sub.tierCode + ', paid ' + sub.pricePaid + ')');

header.description: Generated from {{$guid}} by this request's pre-request script and reused by the replay request.

request.description:
Subscribes a user to a plan at a tier via the reserve -> charge -> activate flow. Responds **201 Created** with `SubscriptionDto{id, userId, planCadence, tierCode, status, pricePaid, trialEnd, startAt, expiresAt}`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#req.userId().toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`, checked against the `userId` in the **body**. Without it any token holder could subscribe on another member's behalf and charge them.

Body is exactly `SubscribeRequest{userId, planId, tierId}` — `userId` is `@NotNull @Positive`, the other two `@NotNull`. There is no coupon field.

Subscribing at SILVER always works: SILVER carries no tier criteria, which is what makes it the unconditional base tier. Asking for GOLD/PLATINUM without qualifying activity returns **422**.

`Idempotency-Key` is optional but sent here: replaying the same key returns the original subscription instead of creating a second one. The pre-request script seeds it from `{{$guid}}` and stores it so the next request can replay it.

Captures `subscriptionId`.

header.description: Generated from {{$guid}} by this request's pre-request script and reused by the replay request.

originalRequest.description:
Subscribes a user to a plan at a tier via the reserve -> charge -> activate flow. Responds **201 Created** with `SubscriptionDto{id, userId, planCadence, tierCode, status, pricePaid, trialEnd, startAt, expiresAt}`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#req.userId().toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`, checked against the `userId` in the **body**. Without it any token holder could subscribe on another member's behalf and charge them.

Body is exactly `SubscribeRequest{userId, planId, tierId}` — `userId` is `@NotNull @Positive`, the other two `@NotNull`. There is no coupon field.

Subscribing at SILVER always works: SILVER carries no tier criteria, which is what makes it the unconditional base tier. Asking for GOLD/PLATINUM without qualifying activity returns **422**.

`Idempotency-Key` is optional but sent here: replaying the same key returns the original subscription instead of creating a second one. The pre-request script seeds it from `{{$guid}}` and stores it so the next request can replay it.

Captures `subscriptionId`.

exec: pm.test('201 Created — replayed, not re-executed', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); });
pm.test('same subscription id as the original call', function () {

header.description: Deliberately the same key the previous request used.

request.description:
Sends the identical payload with the identical `Idempotency-Key` as the previous request — the retry a flaky network would produce.

**Auth: self-or-admin** (same rule as Subscribe).

Expected: the stored result is replayed, so the response is the **same subscription id**, not a second subscription and not a 409. Compare with `Error contract > 409 double-subscribe`, which sends a *fresh* key and is correctly rejected by the `uq_active_subscription_per_user` partial unique index.

header.description: Deliberately the same key the previous request used.

originalRequest.description:
Sends the identical payload with the identical `Idempotency-Key` as the previous request — the retry a flaky network would produce.

**Auth: self-or-admin** (same rule as Subscribe).

Expected: the stored result is replayed, so the response is the **same subscription id**, not a second subscription and not a 409. Compare with `Error contract > 409 double-subscribe`, which sends a *fresh* key and is correctly rejected by the `uq_active_subscription_per_user` partial unique index.

request.description:
The "current membership + expiry" view: plan, tier code/name/rank, the resolved benefits the tier unlocks, lifecycle status, `pricePaid`, `trialEnd`, `startAt` and `expiresAt`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#userId.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

A user with no subscription gets an inactive view (`active: false`, `status: "NONE"`, empty benefits) rather than a 404. Run this after feeding activity to see the tier — and the benefit list — change.

response.name: 200 — membership + benefits

originalRequest.description:
The "current membership + expiry" view: plan, tier code/name/rank, the resolved benefits the tier unlocks, lifecycle status, `pricePaid`, `trialEnd`, `startAt` and `expiresAt`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#userId.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

A user with no subscription gets an inactive view (`active: false`, `status: "NONE"`, empty benefits) rather than a 404. Run this after feeding activity to see the tier — and the benefit list — change.

header.description: Fresh key per send; reuse one to make a retry replay instead of re-mutating.

request.description:
Moves the subscription to a higher tier. Eligibility is enforced against the tier criteria and the write is optimistic-locked (`@Version`), so a lost race surfaces as **409**, not a 500.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("@subscriptionServiceImpl.isOwner(#id, authentication.name) or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

Body is `ChangeTierRequest{targetTierId}` (`@NotNull`).

Run `Activity > Record order activity` first: the seeded PLATINUM criteria are 15+ lifetime orders **or** 20000 monthly spend **or** the `VIP` cohort. Upgrading while unqualified returns **422 Business Rule Violation** as problem+json — see `Error contract`.

header.description: Fresh key per send; reuse one to make a retry replay instead of re-mutating.

originalRequest.description:
Moves the subscription to a higher tier. Eligibility is enforced against the tier criteria and the write is optimistic-locked (`@Version`), so a lost race surfaces as **409**, not a 500.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("@subscriptionServiceImpl.isOwner(#id, authentication.name) or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

Body is `ChangeTierRequest{targetTierId}` (`@NotNull`).

Run `Activity > Record order activity` first: the seeded PLATINUM criteria are 15+ lifetime orders **or** 20000 monthly spend **or** the `VIP` cohort. Upgrading while unqualified returns **422 Business Rule Violation** as problem+json — see `Error contract`.

request.description:
Moves the subscription to a lower tier. A downgrade is the member's own choice, so no eligibility check applies. There is no mid-cycle refund — the lower price takes effect at renewal (a documented policy choice).

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — same `isOwner` rule as upgrade.

Body is `ChangeTierRequest{targetTierId}`. Tier changes are only permitted while the subscription is `ACTIVE` (`SubscriptionStatus.isModifiable()`); attempting one on a `CANCELLED` or `EXPIRED` subscription returns **409**.

originalRequest.description:
Moves the subscription to a lower tier. A downgrade is the member's own choice, so no eligibility check applies. There is no mid-cycle refund — the lower price takes effect at renewal (a documented policy choice).

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — same `isOwner` rule as upgrade.

Body is `ChangeTierRequest{targetTierId}`. Tier changes are only permitted while the subscription is `ACTIVE` (`SubscriptionStatus.isModifiable()`); attempting one on a `CANCELLED` or `EXPIRED` subscription returns **409**.

request.description:
Cancels the subscription and issues a credit note for the unused time. No request body.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — without this check any token holder could cancel any subscription by id.

`CANCELLED` is terminal, so a second cancel (or a refund afterwards) returns **409 Illegal Subscription State** as problem+json. Run `List credit notes` next to see the credit this produced.

originalRequest.description:
Cancels the subscription and issues a credit note for the unused time. No request body.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — without this check any token holder could cancel any subscription by id.

`CANCELLED` is terminal, so a second cancel (or a refund afterwards) returns **409 Illegal Subscription State** as problem+json. Run `List credit notes` next to see the credit this produced.

request.description:
Refunds / charges back the subscription: revokes access, marks the payment refunded in the ledger and issues a credit note. No request body.

**Auth: ADMIN only** — deliberately *not* owner-or-admin. Refund is an ops action; owner-or-admin would let any member refund themselves at will.

This is an **alternative** terminal action to cancel, not a follow-on: if you already ran `Cancel subscription` on this `subscriptionId`, this returns **409 Illegal Subscription State**. To exercise it, subscribe again first.

originalRequest.description:
Refunds / charges back the subscription: revokes access, marks the payment refunded in the ledger and issues a credit note. No request body.

**Auth: ADMIN only** — deliberately *not* owner-or-admin. Refund is an ops action; owner-or-admin would let any member refund themselves at will.

This is an **alternative** terminal action to cancel, not a follow-on: if you already ran `Cancel subscription` on this `subscriptionId`, this returns **409 Illegal Subscription State**. To exercise it, subscribe again first.

request.description:
Lists the credits issued to a user for unused time on cancel or refund. Returns `CreditNoteDto{id, subscriptionId, userId, amount, reason, createdAt}`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#userId.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

Empty until a cancel or refund has run.

originalRequest.description:
Lists the credits issued to a user for unused time on cancel or refund. Returns `CreditNoteDto{id, subscriptionId, userId, amount, reason, createdAt}`.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — `@PreAuthorize("#userId.toString() == authentication.name or hasRole('ADMIN')")`.

Empty until a cancel or refund has run.

item.name: Activity
item.description: Order-activity ingestion — a service-to-service surface, not a user-facing one.

exec: console.log('tier is now ' + view.tierCode + ' with ' + (view.benefits || []).length + ' benefits');
// Reuse one id for this send so the Refund sibling can target the same order.

request.description:
Records one order event and re-evaluates the user's tier, returning the updated `MembershipView`.

Spend is per-order: the order lands in `user_order_event`, and the month's `user_monthly_spend` bucket is recomputed as a SUM over the events. `orderId` is the idempotency key, so sending this request twice does not double-count. `occurredAt` decides which month the spend counts toward, not the server's clock. `orderCount` and `cohorts` are lifetime snapshots.

`{{$timestamp}}` gives a unique order id and `{{$isoTimestamp}}` a current-month timestamp, so the request is runnable as-is. A tier qualifies when any configured criterion is met (OR): GOLD needs 5+ orders / 5000 in a month / `PREMIER`; PLATINUM needs 15+ orders / 20000 in a month / `VIP`.

Auth: ADMIN only. Order activity is asserted by the order service, never by the member it describes, or a member could self-upgrade to the top tier while renewal keeps billing the locked-in `pricePaid`.

response.name: 200 — auto-upgraded to PLATINUM

originalRequest.description:
Records one order event and re-evaluates the user's tier, returning the updated `MembershipView`.

Spend is per-order: the order lands in `user_order_event`, and the month's `user_monthly_spend` bucket is recomputed as a SUM over the events. `orderId` is the idempotency key, so sending this request twice does not double-count. `occurredAt` decides which month the spend counts toward, not the server's clock. `orderCount` and `cohorts` are lifetime snapshots.

`{{$timestamp}}` gives a unique order id and `{{$isoTimestamp}}` a current-month timestamp, so the request is runnable as-is. A tier qualifies when any configured criterion is met (OR): GOLD needs 5+ orders / 5000 in a month / `PREMIER`; PLATINUM needs 15+ orders / 20000 in a month / `VIP`.

Auth: ADMIN only. Order activity is asserted by the order service, never by the member it describes, or a member could self-upgrade to the top tier while renewal keeps billing the locked-in `pricePaid`.

request.description: Corrects an order by re-sending the same `orderId` with a lower `orderAmount`. This is how a refund works: the event row is updated in place and the month's bucket is recomputed downward. A pre-aggregated month total could not express this. Run `Record order activity` first (its pre-request script stores that order's id in `refundOrderId`), then run this to see the spend drop.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Runtime configuration of the benefit catalog and per-tier perks. All of `/api/v1/admin/**` is `hasRole('ADMIN')` in SecurityConfig. Perks are data, not code — these endpoints re-tune what a tier unlocks with no deploy.

request.description:
Adds a benefit to the catalog. Responds **201 Created** with `BenefitDto{id, code, type, name, description, active}`.

**Auth: ADMIN.**

Body is `CreateBenefitRequest{code, type, name, description}`. `code` and `name` are `@NotBlank`; `type` is `@NotNull` and must be one of the five `BenefitType` values: `FREE_DELIVERY`, `EXTRA_DISCOUNT`, `EXCLUSIVE_DEALS`, `EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS`, `PRIORITY_SUPPORT`. `description` is optional.

A benefit is inert until it is attached to a tier — run `Assign a benefit to a tier` next.

response.name: 201 — perk created at runtime

originalRequest.description:
Adds a benefit to the catalog. Responds **201 Created** with `BenefitDto{id, code, type, name, description, active}`.

**Auth: ADMIN.**

Body is `CreateBenefitRequest{code, type, name, description}`. `code` and `name` are `@NotBlank`; `type` is `@NotNull` and must be one of the five `BenefitType` values: `FREE_DELIVERY`, `EXTRA_DISCOUNT`, `EXCLUSIVE_DEALS`, `EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS`, `PRIORITY_SUPPORT`. `description` is optional.

A benefit is inert until it is attached to a tier — run `Assign a benefit to a tier` next.

response.body:
{
  "id": 6,
  "code": "BIRTHDAY_COUPONS",
  "type": "EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS",
  "name": "Birthday Coupons",
  "description": "Bonus tier-only coupons released in the member's birthday month",
  "active": true
}

item.name: Assign / re-tune a benefit on a tier

request.description:
Attaches a benefit to a tier, or re-tunes the params of an existing attachment. Responds **204 No Content** and evicts the catalog caches, so `GET /api/v1/tiers` reflects the change immediately.

**Auth: ADMIN.**

Body is `AssignBenefitRequest{tierId, benefitCode, params}`: `tierId` is `@NotNull`, `benefitCode` is `@NotBlank`, `params` is a free-form JSON object whose shape depends on the benefit type — `{"minOrderValue":0}` for FREE_DELIVERY, `{"discountPct":15}` for EXTRA_DISCOUNT, `{"couponsPerCycle":5}` for EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS, `{}` for EXCLUSIVE_DEALS and PRIORITY_SUPPORT.

The example above raises Platinum's extra discount from the seeded 15% to 20%. An unknown `tierId` or `benefitCode` returns **404** as problem+json.

originalRequest.description:
Attaches a benefit to a tier, or re-tunes the params of an existing attachment. Responds **204 No Content** and evicts the catalog caches, so `GET /api/v1/tiers` reflects the change immediately.

**Auth: ADMIN.**

Body is `AssignBenefitRequest{tierId, benefitCode, params}`: `tierId` is `@NotNull`, `benefitCode` is `@NotBlank`, `params` is a free-form JSON object whose shape depends on the benefit type — `{"minOrderValue":0}` for FREE_DELIVERY, `{"discountPct":15}` for EXTRA_DISCOUNT, `{"couponsPerCycle":5}` for EXCLUSIVE_COUPONS, `{}` for EXCLUSIVE_DEALS and PRIORITY_SUPPORT.

The example above raises Platinum's extra discount from the seeded 15% to 20%. An unknown `tierId` or `benefitCode` returns **404** as problem+json.

item.name: Unassign a benefit from a tier

request.description:
Removes a benefit from a tier — the inverse of `Assign / re-tune a benefit on a tier`, which attached EXTRA_DISCOUNT to Platinum. Responds **204 No Content** and evicts the catalog caches, so `GET /api/v1/tiers` no longer lists that perk on the tier. Run it straight after Assign for a clean assign -> unassign round-trip on the same tier and benefit.

**Auth: ADMIN** — `/api/v1/admin/**` is `hasRole('ADMIN')`.

Path params are the numeric `tierId` and the benefit `code`. An unknown tier, or a benefit not currently attached to it, returns **404** as problem+json.

item.name: Actuator
item.description: Operational endpoints. Only the liveness slice is exposed and anonymous: `/actuator/health`, `/actuator/health/**` and `/actuator/info` are `permitAll()`. Nothing else under `/actuator` is exposed (`management.endpoints.web.exposure.include` is `health,info`); the in-process counters (`membership.lifecycle`, payment-failure) stay in the meter registry. Any endpoint enabled later is ADMIN by default, never accidentally public.

request.description:
Liveness / readiness aggregate across Postgres and the disk. 

**Auth: public** — no token sent. Details are `show-details: when-authorized`, so send an admin token if you want the per-component breakdown.

originalRequest.description:

**Auth: public** — no token sent. Details are `show-details: when-authorized`, so send an admin token if you want the per-component breakdown.

request.description:
Build / application info.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

originalRequest.description:
Build / application info.

**Auth: public** — no token sent.

item.name: Error contract
item.description:
Negative paths that demonstrate the error contract. **Every response in this folder is RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`** with `type`, `title`, `status` and `detail` — 404 not found, 409 conflict (including concurrency losses and illegal lifecycle transitions), 422 domain-rule violation, 402 payment declined, 400 bean-validation failure.

Run these after the happy path, while the user still has an ACTIVE subscription.

request.description:
Subscribes with a `planId` that does not exist.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — authorization passes (the body `userId` is the token subject); the request fails on the domain lookup, not the security layer.

Expected **404 Not Found**, returned as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json` with `title: "Not Found"` and a `type` of `https://firstclub.com/problems/not-found`. No `Idempotency-Key` is sent so the call is never replayed from the store.

originalRequest.description:
Subscribes with a `planId` that does not exist.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — authorization passes (the body `userId` is the token subject); the request fails on the domain lookup, not the security layer.

Expected **404 Not Found**, returned as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json` with `title: "Not Found"` and a `type` of `https://firstclub.com/problems/not-found`. No `Idempotency-Key` is sent so the call is never replayed from the store.

exec: pm.test('RFC-7807 problem+json, Business Rule Violation', function () {

request.description:
Asks to be upgraded to PLATINUM without the qualifying activity. Send this **before** `Activity > Record order activity` — once that has run the user genuinely qualifies and this returns 200.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — the request is authorized; it is the *domain rule* that rejects it, which is exactly why the status is 422 and not 403.

Expected **422 Unprocessable Entity** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`, `title: "Business Rule Violation"`. A 422 means the request was well-formed and permitted but a domain rule said no — distinct from 400 (malformed) and 409 (state the caller can fix by reloading and retrying).

response.name: 422 — problem+json

originalRequest.description:
Asks to be upgraded to PLATINUM without the qualifying activity. Send this **before** `Activity > Record order activity` — once that has run the user genuinely qualifies and this returns 200.

**Auth: owner-or-admin** — the request is authorized; it is the *domain rule* that rejects it, which is exactly why the status is 422 and not 403.

Expected **422 Unprocessable Entity** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`, `title: "Business Rule Violation"`. A 422 means the request was well-formed and permitted but a domain rule said no — distinct from 400 (malformed) and 409 (state the caller can fix by reloading and retrying).

response.body:
{
  "detail": "User 7001 has not met the criteria for tier PLATINUM",
  "instance": "/api/v1/subscriptions/1/upgrade",
  "status": 422,
  "title": "Business Rule Violation",
  "type": "https://firstclub.com/problems/business-rule-violation"
}

header.description: A FRESH key on purpose — this must be treated as a new request, not a replay.

request.description:
Subscribes a second time while the user already holds an ACTIVE subscription. Requires the happy-path `Subscribe` to have run and no cancel/refund since.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — authorization passes; the conflict is a state conflict.

Expected **409 Conflict** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`. The guarantee is enforced at the **database** layer by the `uq_active_subscription_per_user` partial unique index, not by an application-level check, so it holds under concurrency too — a `DataIntegrityViolationException` is translated to 409 rather than a 500.

Note the deliberately **fresh** `Idempotency-Key`: reusing the original key would replay the first result instead (see `Subscriptions > Subscribe — replay the same Idempotency-Key`). Same endpoint, same body — the key is what separates "a retry" from "a duplicate".

header.description: A FRESH key on purpose — this must be treated as a new request, not a replay.

originalRequest.description:
Subscribes a second time while the user already holds an ACTIVE subscription. Requires the happy-path `Subscribe` to have run and no cancel/refund since.

**Auth: self-or-admin** — authorization passes; the conflict is a state conflict.

Expected **409 Conflict** as RFC-7807 `application/problem+json`. The guarantee is enforced at the **database** layer by the `uq_active_subscription_per_user` partial unique index, not by an application-level check, so it holds under concurrency too — a `DataIntegrityViolationException` is translated to 409 rather than a 500.

Note the deliberately **fresh** `Idempotency-Key`: reusing the original key would replay the first result instead (see `Subscriptions > Subscribe — replay the same Idempotency-Key`). Same endpoint, same body — the key is what separates "a retry" from "a duplicate".
